calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-25-2002 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance or an opposition filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 225. Proceedings reported by: Leticia Perez. (212)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-09-2002 Case Settled - Set For Crc 525 Hearing On Nov-19-02 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk Nicole Adams, Reporter Beldon Yee.

Case called from trial. Case settled, terms of the settlement placed on the record in Dept. 206.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael Meheen, Esq. 831/649-3600 for cross-defendants A.R. CONSTRUCTION, et al. moving for summary judgment
David Bona, Esq. of THORNTON TAYLOR 421-8890 for cross-complainant MAXIM CRANE opposing party
* A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- Cross-Defendants A.R. Construction, et al.'s Motion for Summary Judgment: DENIED. Triable Issue of Fact regarding enforceability of indemnity agreement. Hon. A. JAMES ROBERTSON II (301/) Joyce Long, CSR 8620

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-24-2002 Continued To Sep-09-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Moiton For A Separate Trial Of The Claims Against It; Stipulation In Regard To Severance
Off Calendar Jun-13-2002 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Meheen, Esq., 831-649-3600, appeared on behalf of cross-defendant and cross-complainant A.R. Construction. Otto Friedrich Becker, Esq., THORNTON, TAYLOR, DOWNS & BECKER, 415-421-8890, appeared on behalf of cross-complainant and cross-defendant Maxim Crane Works. John Cyril Smith, Esq., 415-861-1886 appeared on behalf of plaintiff Luis Vizcarra.

Parties stipulated on the record: that they would not oppose a sixty or more day continuance of the trial date; parties stipulate that they will not oppose A.R. CONSTRUCTION's Motion for Summary Judgment being heard on shortened time; and If expert discovery is required that A.R. CONSTRUCTION may appeal to the Court for relief. The tentative ruling was adopted as the order of the court: Cross-complainant A.R. CONSTRUCTION's Motion for a separate trial of the claims against, and stipulation in regard to severence: Motion to sever: Denied - common issues should be tried in single trial. (AJR) Carol Karen CSR#8189

Motion For Leave To File A Xcomplaint; Memorandum Of P/A And Declaration In Support
Off Calendar Mar-12-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: N O A P P E A R A N C E. Tentative ruling adopted -- Grant defendant MAXIM CRANE WORKS's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint. (JJM/md) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

STATUS CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2001 Set For Trial On Jun-24-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jun-04-02 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 220
